fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to michigan, respiratory therapist compact michigan, michigan respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Michigan LARA — Respiratory Therapy
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)
board.mailing_address:
Bureau of Professional Licensing
PO Box 30670
Lansing, MI 48909

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Michigan.

steps.title: Register for a MiPLUS Account
steps.description: Navigate to the MiPLUS online portal (michigan.gov/miplus) and select 'Register for an Account' to create your user profile.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Log in to your MiPLUS account, select 'New Application', and choose 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the list of professions. Select the appropriate application type (e.g., initial licensure or endorsement).

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form, including personal information, education, and examination details (NBRC).

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documents
steps.description: Digitally upload all necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts from your approved respiratory care program, NBRC score reports, and a consent for a criminal background check.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the required application and initial license fees ($185 for initial/endorsement) securely through the MiPLUS system using a credit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: Monitor your MiPLUS account for updates. The Michigan Board of Respiratory Care will review your application and documents. An email notification will be sent once a decision is made or if additional information is required.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Michigan respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Michigan
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Michigan, submit your application online through the MiPLUS portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MiPLUS portal and click 'Register for an Account'. Follow the prompts to create your user ID and password. Once registered, log in to access the application dashboard.
